habitat: Mountain coniferous/mixed forest; forest edge; deciduous/coniferous/riparian woodland
story: In the diverse world of birds, the Japanese Sparrowhawk (Accipiter gularis) stands out. This species, part of the Accipitridae family, is a resident of Mountain coniferous/mixed forest; forest edge; deciduous/coniferous/riparian woodland. Its IUCN Red List status is Least Concern (LC), which serves as a call to action for habitat preservation. The Japanese Sparrowhawk is more than just a bird; it is a symbol of the rich biodiversity that we must protect. Through this poster, we hope to foster a greater understanding and appreciation for the Japanese Sparrowhawk and its role in the ecosystem.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
